title: Make RepoBrief publication identity-bound and retention-bounded
goal: Ensure identical repository commit, profile, configuration, Lenskit version and schema produce no duplicate full publication, then retain bounded history and explicit pins in a managed artifact store.

approval.note: Source changes and read-only verification are authorized. Runtime deletion or cleanup effects require a fresh exact-target plan and post-readback.

acceptance.assertion: A repeated publication with identical canonical identity writes no new full artifact directory.
Per repository and lane, the last three full artifacts, seven daily anchors, eight weekly anchors and explicit pins are retained.
Incomplete or failed publication generations expire after 48 hours unless pinned for debugging.
Durable manifests and hashes are separated from large regenerable payloads.
Crash recovery, concurrent publication and retention selection are deterministic and covered.

metadata.source: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1 plan from live 2026-07-14 storage audit
does_not_establish: permission to bypass leases or dirty-state guards
metadata.readiness_basis: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1-T003 verified by merged Grabowski PR #232, deployed exact merge identity and live preflight/grace/limit evidence.
validation: 4,067 full tests passed with one expected skip and 13 explicit deselections on the published implementation.
All substantive GitHub checks passed, including pytest-full, ruff, browser tests, CodeQL, contracts, documentation and repository guards.

verified_acceptance.identity-noop: Canonical repository, profile, configuration, Lenskit and schema identity is persisted; an identical publication reuses the existing complete generation instead of writing another full artifact directory.
verified_acceptance.retention: Selection preserves the last three full artifacts, seven daily anchors, eight weekly anchors and explicit pins per repository and lane.
verified_acceptance.incomplete-ttl: Incomplete and failed generations expire after 48 hours unless explicitly pinned for debugging.
verified_acceptance.evidence-split: Small durable manifests, hashes and receipts remain separate from large regenerable publication payloads.
verified_acceptance.tests: Crash recovery, concurrent publication, identity no-op and deterministic retention selection are covered by the merged full and focused suites.

completion.outcome: RepoBrief publication now avoids duplicate complete generations for identical identities and retains bounded history, incomplete generations and explicit pins under deterministic policy.
